Title: Insights into the missing apiosylation step in flavonoid apiosides biosynthesis of Leguminosae plants

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase (UGT); GT-B fold; 45-aa PSPG box with RLGSDH motif
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots of G. uralensis; co-expressed with GuCHS and GuCHR; lower in cortex and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in infiltrated leaves of N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in tobacco; exogenous UDP-GlcA and/or isoliquiritin or isoliquiritigenin feeding in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Liquiritin; isoliquiritin; diverse flavonoid/lignan/coumarin glycosides; sugar donor UDP-apiose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to the 2''-hydroxyl of sugar moieties on flavonoid glycosides, forming β-2''-O-apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Missing terminal determinant for flavonoid apioside formation; key terminal apiosylation step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Liquiritin apioside; isoliquiritin apioside; flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Liquiritin apioside is a bioactive licorice constituent with potent antitussive activity; flavonoid apiosides are believed to play hormone-like roles in plant growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of G. uralensis; engineered leaves of N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes efficient and regioselective accumulation of flavonoid 2''-O-apiosides; enabled de novo biosynthesis of at least 10 flavonoid apiosides in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; RT-PCR cloning; recombinant protein enzyme assays; LC/MS and MS2; kinetics; substrate screening; crystal structure; mutagenesis; MD/metadynamics/QM-MM; transient expression in N. benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: GuGT14 in pathway reconstruction caused byproduct formation due to poor regioselectivity, whereas GuApiGT itself showed broad acceptor promiscuity but strict donor specificity; no explicit growth penalty reported in tobacco transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuApiGT (MSTRG.23171.4/UGT79B74) was identified from G. uralensis as the missing phenolic apiosyltransferase for flavonoid apioside biosynthesis. 2. It efficiently and regioselectively catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of liquiritin, isoliquiritin, and many other phenolic glycosides, with strict selectivity for UDP-apiose. 3. Structural and mutational analyses showed the unusual 45-aa PSPG box and RLGSDH motif underlie UDP-apiose selectivity. 4. Introduction of GuApiGT with upstream genes into N. benthamiana enabled de novo production of liquiritin apioside, isoliquiritin apioside, and additional flavonoid apiosides.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure PDB 8HZZ. Key residues implicated in sugar donor recognition include R368, S371, D372, H373, and I136; H18/D115 implicated in catalysis. Mutants altered sugar donor selectivity toward UDP-Xyl or UDP-Glc.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for metabolic engineering of flavonoid apiosides; suitable for pathway stacking with UAXS and regioselective upstream glycosyltransferases such as GuGT53 in plant chassis; useful scaffold for engineering donor specificity.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; 45-aa PSPG box; RLGSDY-like motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as an apiosyltransferase from Leguminosae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and enzyme catalysis reactions with LC/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtApiGT was cloned from P. thomsonii based on the conserved 45-aa PSPG box. 2. Enzyme assays confirmed PtApiGT functions as an apiosyltransferase. 3. Its characterization supports the generality of the Leguminosae apiosyltransferase motif.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four additional Leguminosae ApiGTs identified in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme resource for engineering flavonoid apioside production in legumes or heterologous systems.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; 45-aa PSPG box; RLGSDH motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as an apiosyltransferase from Leguminosae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis/cloning and enzyme catalysis reactions with LC/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsApiGT was identified among Leguminosae candidates using the unique PSPG-box signature. 2. Enzyme assays verified SsApiGT as a flavonoid apiosyltransferase. 3. This supports conservation of the RLGSDH-associated apiosylation mechanism in legumes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full-length gene was synthesized for characterization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful apiosyltransferase alternative for synthetic biology and comparative engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; 45-aa PSPG box; RLGSDH motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as an apiosyltransferase from licorice relative G. glabra
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and enzyme catalysis reactions with LC/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GgApiGT was cloned from G. glabra and functionally confirmed as an apiosyltransferase. 2. Its activity supports conservation of flavonoid apioside biosynthesis in Glycyrrhiza species. 3. The enzyme belongs to the Leguminosae-specific ApiGT clade containing the 45-aa PSPG box.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative ApiGT for licorice metabolic engineering and comparative functional studies.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; 45-aa PSPG box; RLGSDH motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as an apiosyltransferase from G. inflata
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and enzyme catalysis reactions with LC/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GiApiGT was identified and cloned from G. inflata. 2. Enzyme assays verified its apiosyltransferase activity. 3. This extends the conserved Leguminosae ApiGT family to another Glycyrrhiza species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme source for engineering apioside biosynthesis in heterologous hosts.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar expression pattern to GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in infiltrated leaves of N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regioselectively glycosylates liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin at 4'/4-O to form the glucoside acceptors for downstream apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream glycosylation step supplying apiosylation acceptors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improved de novo production of isoliquiritin apioside by providing regioselective precursor glucosides and reducing byproduct formation relative to GuGT14
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; gene cloning; enzyme assay; transient pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: None reported; selected to avoid byproduct 7a generated when GuGT14 was used because of poor regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuGT53 was discovered from G. uralensis as a co-expressed UGT. 2. It efficiently and regioselectively glycosylates liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin to liquiritin and isoliquiritin. 3. Replacing GuGT14 with GuGT53 improved the yield of isoliquiritin apioside in engineered tobacco by minimizing off-pathway glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in module 3 of the tobacco reconstruction to supply correct glycosylated acceptors for GuApiGT.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important companion enzyme for stacking with GuApiGT in synthetic pathways aimed at producing liquiritin apioside or isoliquiritin apioside.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in infiltrated leaves of N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Used in enzymatic assays with NAD+ and UDP-GlcA; transient co-expression in tobacco to supplement UDP-apiose donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucuronic acid into UDP-apiose and UDP-xylose, supplying the apiose donor pool
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: UDP-apiose biosynthesis / sugar donor supply for flavonoid apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key donor-supply step in the engineered system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports biosynthesis of bioactive flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled GuApiGT-catalyzed apiosylation by supplying UDP-apiose in vitro and in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coupled enzyme assays; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Also produces UDP-xylose, but GuApiGT wild type did not utilize UDP-xylose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis UAXS was required to generate UDP-apiose because the donor is not commercially available. 2. In vitro it supplied UDP-apiose for GuApiGT assays. 3. In tobacco, co-expression of UAXS was necessary to obtain flavonoid apiosides de novo.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background enzyme for native apiose synthesis but experimentally essential in this study’s functional assays and heterologous pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential donor-supply module to combine with apiosyltransferases in heterologous production platforms.
